screens.title: Welcome to Neighbourpura
description: Neighbourpura is a colony in an Indian city. Its residents work in Citynagar. Some walk, some drive, and some take the bus.

The road connecting Neighbourpura to the Citynagar main road is in poor condition - dug up for pipelines, wiring, sewerage, and construction, with no footpaths. One day, some Neighbourpura residents decide to get the road fixed.
What will you do?
screens.imageAlt: Residents standing near a badly damaged road in their colony

actions.label: Encourage the residents to go ahead
Tell them there is no point

screens.title: A Broken Road, A Broken Leg

revealBlocks.text: You continue using the broken road. One day, you trip on a stone and break your leg. You miss work for a week and spend ₹5,000 on medical care.

screens.imageAlt: A resident injured after tripping on a damaged road

actions.label: Complain to the residents petitioning for a better road

screens.title: You Move On
description: Your leg heals. You're more careful now. You know which patches to avoid.
The others are still at it. You wish them luck. Maybe it'll work out, maybe it won't.
screens.imageAlt: A resident walking carefully past potholes on a familiar route

screens.title: About This Game
description: This simulation is designed to show how difficult it can be for citizens to fix a simple city road in their neighbourhood. Would you like to learn about the reforms Janaagraha advocates to address the core systemic issues in our cities?
screens.imageAlt: An informational screen introducing the purpose of the simulation

screens.title: The Residents Invite You
description: You reach out to the residents' group and they invite you to join them.
screens.imageAlt: Residents inviting a neighbour to join their effort

screens.title: The Residents Fill You In
description: The residents update you and explain that they plan to approach the Councillor.
screens.imageAlt: Residents briefing a new volunteer about the next step

screens.title: Two Weeks Have Passed
description: It has been two weeks since you joined the group working to get the road fixed. The situation remains unchanged.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Residents waiting for progress that has not come

actions.label: Check in with the residents' group

screens.title: You Cheered Them On
description: You encouraged the residents to go ahead and hope they succeed.

revealBlocks.text: It has been two weeks since you encouraged the group wanting to fix the road.

screens.imageAlt: A neighbour watching others take up the cause

actions.label: Check in with the residents' group

screens.title: Still Waiting on the MLA
description: You learn that the group has approached the MLA but has yet to receive a response.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: Residents waiting for a response from the MLA

actions.label: Suggest they approach the Councillor instead

screens.title: Word on the Street
description: You hear something from someone who heard it from someone else - the kind of information that may or may not be reliable.
screens.imageAlt: Neighbours exchanging uncertain information in the street

actions.label: Talk to the residents' group to verify

screens.title: The Group Comes Together
description: A group of ten residents has come together. You meet to discuss next steps and decide on a plan of action.

revealBlocks.text: What plan of action will you recommend?

screens.imageAlt: Residents gathered together to discuss a strategy

screens.title: Out of the MLA's Hands
description: The MLA hears you out but explains that road repairs in a residential colony fall under the Municipal Corporation. He's not the right person for this.
screens.imageAlt: An MLA explaining that the matter falls under the Municipal Corporation

actions.label: Seek help from the MLA to reach out to the Councillor

screens.title: Nobody Knows Where the Councillor Sits
description: The group sets out to meet the Councillor, but nobody knows the location of their office.
screens.imageAlt: Residents trying to figure out where the Councillor's office is

actions.label: You search for the office address online
Visit the Citynagar Municipal office for help

screens.title: Complaint Registered, No Follow-Up
description: Your complaint is successfully registered online, but there is no acknowledgement or follow-up after submission.
screens.imageAlt: An online complaint form with no further response

screens.title: The MLA Connects You to the Councillor
description: The MLA puts you in touch with the Councillor directly.
screens.imageAlt: An MLA connecting residents to the Councillor's office

screens.title: The Office Address Is Wrong
description: You find an address online, but when you visit, the Councillor doesn't sit there. Perhaps the residents' group knows more about the Councillor's whereabouts.
screens.imageAlt: A resident arriving at the wrong address for the Councillor

actions.label: Meet the residents' group for more information

screens.title: The Group Has a Suggestion - Ask at the Municipal Office
description: The residents' group doesn't know the Councillor's exact whereabouts either, but someone suggests trying the municipal office.
screens.imageAlt: Residents suggesting the municipal office as the next lead

screens.title: The Municipal Office Has the Details
description: Unlike the internet, the municipal office staff have the right details. You leave with the Councillor's correct address in hand.
screens.imageAlt: Municipal office staff sharing the correct Councillor details

screens.title: You Finally Reach the Councillor
description: All roads lead to the Councillor. You finally arrive at their office. But they are unavailable today. They promise to meet you on Sunday afternoon.
What will you do?
screens.imageAlt: Residents finally reaching the Councillor's office

screens.title: The Councillor Hears You Out
description: You return on Sunday. The Councillor greets you and listens to your concern. She says she will inform the Assistant Engineer (AE). Will you wait for the AE to visit your colony?
screens.imageAlt: The Councillor listening to the residents' concern

actions.label: Wait a few days for the AE's visit

screens.title: The AE Visits and Then It Goes Quiet
description: A few days later, the Assistant Engineer (AE) visits your colony to assess the damage. He takes note of what needs to be done and leaves. Days pass, but nothing happens.
screens.imageAlt: An Assistant Engineer inspecting the road and leaving

actions.label: Go back to the Councillor's office

screens.title: Try the Ward Office
description: The Councillor asks you to visit the Ward Office for your area and follow up there.
screens.imageAlt: Residents redirected to the Ward Office

screens.title: The Group Loses Hope
description: The AE never returns. No one knows what happened. The residents' group has lost motivation and quietly disbanded.
screens.imageAlt: A disheartened residents' group losing momentum

screens.title: Progress at the Ward Office
description: You meet the AE at the Ward Office. He explains that the required workers are currently unavailable, but assures you the work will be completed within the next week. A few days later, a team from the Citynagar Municipal Corporation arrives at the colony to patch sections of the broken road.
screens.imageAlt: Municipal workers patching sections of the broken road

actions.label: Express concern about the quality of patchwork

screens.title: The Patchwork Was Right to Worry About
description: Your concern turns out to be well-founded. A few days later, the patchwork washes away in the rain.
You return to the Councillor to demand an explanation for the poor workmanship. The Councillor summons the AE.
screens.imageAlt: Rain exposing failed patchwork repairs on the road

screens.title: The Rain Washes It All Away
description: Unfortunately, a few days later, the patchwork is washed away by the rain.
You return to the Councillor to demand an explanation for the poor workmanship. The Councillor summons the AE.
screens.imageAlt: Patchwork road repairs washed away after rainfall

screens.title: The Problem Is Bigger Than Patchwork
description: The AE explains that a proper fix will require significant funding, far more than what is available. He says the matter is beyond his authority and asks you to take it up with the Councillor.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: The Assistant Engineer explaining the need for larger funding

actions.label: Demand that the Councillor secure the necessary funds

screens.title: The Councillor Has No Funds Either
description: The Councillor says the decision is not hers to make. The matter must go before the Standing Committee, and that will take time.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: The Councillor explaining that the matter must go to the Standing Committee

actions.label: Demand that the matter be sent to the Standing Committee
Do nothing - everyone drops the matter

screens.title: Referred to the Standing Committee
description: The Councillor agrees to forward your request to the Standing Committee.

revealBlocks.text: Many days pass. You have no idea if there has been any progress.

screens.imageAlt: Time passing while a request sits with the Standing Committee

actions.label: Do nothing - everyone drops the matter

screens.title: The Standing Committee Rejects the Request
description: The Councillor admits she hasn't had a chance to raise the matter yet, but promises to do so that day.
After several follow-ups over the next few weeks, you learn that the Standing Committee has rejected the request citing a shortage of funds. The proposal has been deferred indefinitely.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: A funding request rejected by the Standing Committee

actions.label: Approach the Mayor or Municipal Commissioner
Ask the Councillor to push harder
Do nothing - everyone drops the matter

screens.title: The Mayor Agrees to Help
description: The Mayor and Commissioner listen to your case and agree to speak to the Standing Committee on your behalf.
screens.imageAlt: The Mayor and Commissioner agreeing to help the residents

screens.title: The Councillor Asks You to Wait
description: The Councillor says she will try to raise the matter again when the time is right.
screens.imageAlt: The Councillor asking residents to wait longer

screens.title: A Standing Committee Member Offers No Answers
description: You approach a Standing Committee member. They listen, but give you no satisfactory response.
screens.imageAlt: A Standing Committee member listening without offering help

actions.label: Protest in front of the Municipal Corporation office

screens.title: Other Members Are No Different
description: The other Standing Committee members also give you unsatisfactory responses. You have reached a dead end through this channel.
screens.imageAlt: More committee members offering the same unhelpful response

screens.title: The Standing Committee Approves Funds
description: Your efforts pay off. The Standing Committee agrees to treat this as a priority.

revealBlocks.text: REJOICE! You made all the right moves. The Standing Committee approves funds for the project. You head home hopeful that your road will finally be fixed - and that you will soon be able to walk it without fear.

screens.imageAlt: Residents celebrating after funds are approved

screens.title: No Contractor Will Take the Job
description: Many weeks later, the situation is unchanged. You follow up with the Councillor and the AE, who inform you that a contractor is needed to carry out the work - but none have responded to the tender. The allocated funds are apparently not enough to attract any bids.
What will you do now?
screens.imageAlt: A stalled road project with no contractor in sight

actions.label: Offer to organise the work yourself
Approach the MLA for additional support
Approach the Mayor or Municipal Commissioner
Approach the Standing Committee members again

screens.title: More Funds, A Willing Contractor
description: The Municipal Corporation agrees to release additional funds, making the tender more attractive. A contractor is found and the road is fixed within a couple of weeks.
screens.imageAlt: Road repair work finally moving ahead with a contractor

screens.title: You Can't Do It Yourself
description: You give it your best effort, but the scale and complexity of the work is beyond what you can manage.
screens.imageAlt: A resident realising the road repair is too complex to manage alone

actions.label: Try meeting the Commissioner or the Standing Committee

screens.title: The MLA Comes Through
description: This time, the MLA listens. He arranges for the necessary funds to be sanctioned.
screens.imageAlt: The MLA approving additional support for the road

actions.label: Use the sanctioned funds to fix the road

screens.title: The Road Is Fixed - But for How Long?
description: You did it. Your persistence paid off, and the road is finally fixed. Everyone in the colony is living a more comfortable life.

revealBlocks.text: But a few months later, the sewage department digs up the road to lay pipes for an upcoming real-estate project. Nobody tells you whether it will be restored. What will you do now?

screens.imageAlt: A newly repaired road being dug up again for sewage work

actions.label: Start again and try a different path

screens.title: Choosing a Reform
description: Thank you for stepping up. What kind of systemic reform do you think would make the most difference?
screens.imageAlt: A prompt inviting the player to choose a systemic reform

actions.label: Plan better for roads and utilities to avoid repeated digging
Hire more skilled personnel in the ULB
Give the Municipal Corporation more funds for maintenance
Give the Councillors more power and accountability
Better coordination between the ULB, sewage department, and other agencies

screens.title: Reform: Better Infrastructure Planning
description: The city adopts improved road designs that accommodate utility work without requiring the road to be dug up each time. Once built to this standard, the road would not need repeated disruption.
However, this approach requires significantly more funding. Securing the necessary budget approval will take additional time and effort.
screens.imageAlt: A better planned road and utility layout for the city

screens.title: Reform: Skilled Staff in the ULB
description: With skilled staff in place, the AE is able to tell you upfront that patchwork won't last - and the ULB can carry out proper road repairs without relying on external contractors once sufficient funds are available.
However, the sewage department still digs up the road for the new project, pointing to a deeper coordination problem that this reform alone cannot solve.
screens.imageAlt: Skilled urban local body staff planning road repair work

screens.title: Reform: More Funds for the Municipal Corporation
description: With greater financial resources, the Corporation is able to sanction your road repair much sooner.
However, the sewage department still digs up the road for the new project - pointing to a gap that funding alone cannot bridge.
screens.imageAlt: Municipal funding enabling earlier road repair approvals

screens.title: Reform: Empowering Councillors
description: When elected representatives are given stronger roles in local decision-making, everyday issues like road repairs could move forward more smoothly with fewer procedural hurdles.
screens.imageAlt: Empowered local elected representatives helping residents

screens.title: Reform: Better Inter-Departmental Coordination
description: You still have to go through the full process of securing Standing Committee approval, but this time the road is only built after the sewage pipes are already in place. You wait longer, but the result is a well-built road that is unlikely to be dug up again anytime soon - at least until the next round of utilities work.
screens.imageAlt: Coordinated city departments sequencing utility work before road building

screens.title: Thank You for Trying
description: Thank you for trying to fix Neighbourpura's road. This simulation was designed to show how difficult it can be for citizens to repair a simple city road. You shuttled between offices, waited on funding decisions, and navigated layers of bureaucracy - only for the road to be dug up again a few weeks later.
Each of the reforms you explored has real merit, but none is sufficient on its own. Only when these reforms work together can we meaningfully improve the quality of life in our cities.

That is the case for a City-Systems approach - one that strengthens the core systems running our cities: integrated planning and design, decentralised and participatory governance, and strong financial and organisational capacity.
screens.imageAlt: A closing reflection on the city-systems approach

screens.title: And It Happens Again
description: A few days later, a motorcycle hits a pothole and two people are badly injured. It makes the news briefly, then fades. The road remains broken. And it won't be the last accident.
screens.imageAlt: A dangerous pothole causing another accident on the road

actions.label: Get back in and fight the good fight

screens.title: The Matter Is Dropped
description: Without further follow-up, the issue slowly fades from attention. Residents move on to other concerns, and the road stays broken.
screens.imageAlt: Residents moving on while the road remains broken
